title: Monday.api() with apiVersion 2023-10 gives permission error in board view
excerpt:
I’m trying to develop a new board view and want to use the 2023-10 graphQL schema. 
When setting that version of the API (with the 0.4.X monday-sdk-js package), I am getting the following error in the console: There was …

posts.author: benhowes (Ben Howes 🐨)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m trying to develop a new board view and want to use the 2023-10 graphQL schema.

When setting that version of the API (with the 0.4.X monday-sdk-js package), I am getting the following error in the console: There was an error in response from monday.com graphql API: [{"message":"Permission Denied! Your token doesn't grant access to boards:read"}]

If I update my query to work with the old schema, then the query executes fine.

Is there a way to get this working at the moment?

posts.author: benhowes (Ben Howes 🐨)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve resolved this issue. Correcting the apps permissions resolved the issue.

I’ve not gotten to the bottom of what happened exactly, but my permissions were not set up correctly - I am pretty sure I had tested api calls, but I must’ve done something stupid.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @benhowes and welcome to the community!

Sorry we did not see your message before you found the source of the issue!

I am glad you did

“Obvious mistakes” are the worst. I have spent so much time looking for issues that ended up being a comma, or a missing parenthesis and when you find it is a mix of emotions between “how on earth did I miss thiiiis???” and the relief of knowing why it was failing

Let us know if you need anything else!

Cheers,
Matias
